storyline: NuScale Power (SMR) builds small modular reactors. It rose through mid-2024 on the nuclear-revival trade — AI datacenters scrambling for clean baseload power, and Washington turning pro-nuclear. The move peaked when the ADVANCE Act was signed (July 9, 2024) — the Accelerating Deployment of Versatile, Advanced Nuclear for Clean Energy Act, which streamlines NRC licensing and fees for advanced / small modular reactors. Classic buy-the-rumor / sell-the-news: SMR topped ~July 15, then fell ~44%. What we'd want: exit as the bill is signed (the catalyst resolves) — which the agent did, exiting 2024-07-12 within ~3 days and ~3% of the peak, dodging the crash.

SMR.thesis: NuScale small modular reactor approval amid AI power demand
SMR.exit_case: NuScale's NRC standard design approval was already certified back in Jan 2023 — that specific approval is arguably priced.
SMR.assessment: First week on the NuScale SMR-meets-AI-power thesis. SMR is the only pure-play vehicle in the known set and the cleanest expression. News confirms the AI datacenter power-crunch narrative driving the name while it remains a small-cap not yet broadly hyped, so edge remains. The genuine risk is that the headline 'approval' refers to the 2023 design certification already in hand — meaning the specific approval catalyst could be backward-looking; but the forward catalyst (incremental NRC approvals + first AI-linked deployment contracts) is still pending. Hold with SMR.
SMR.exit_advice: Hold. Entry week; stock still a micro-cap 'under the radar' per ETF.com — far from crowded. Watch for the actual incremental approval (uprated 77MW design / NRC milestone) or a named SMR deployment deal; if that lands and prices, exit.

VST is the cleanest pure-play merchant generator vehicle with large nuclear/gas fleet leverage to PJM pricing. The discrete auction is public, but the multi-year earnings re-rate is forward-looking and still unfolding, so edge remains while the market reprices forward revenue.
VST.exit_advice: Hold. Auction result is announced but the earnings re-rating from elevated capacity payments (2025/26 delivery year) is still being digested — repricing is just beginning, not closed.

SIGA.thesis: WHO declares mpox global health emergency; TPOXX antiviral demand
SIGA.exit_case: WHO declaration already made Aug 15 and stocks spiked same day — event public, possibly priced
SIGA.assessment: Entered on the WHO mpox emergency declaration (made Aug 15). SIGA is the pure-play vehicle for TPOXX antiviral demand — its sole catalyst is government stockpiling/procurement following the emergency. The declaration is the discrete trigger and has occurred, but the demand response (named orders, BARDA/USG procurement) has NOT yet landed, so my edge is in anticipating order flow, not the already-public declaration. First-week sympathy rally is hype-driven (Bavarian Nordic +17%, SIGA moving), not yet backed by orders. Live while procurement is pending.
SIGA.exit_advice: Hold; the declaration triggered the thesis but the actual TPOXX procurement/stockpiling orders that justify SIGA are still pending — watch for government order announcements.

SIGA.thesis: WHO declares mpox global health emergency; TPOXX antiviral demand

The WHO declaration itself is public and priced, but my edge was never the declaration; it was anticipating the TPOXX procurement/stockpiling order flow that follows it, which has NOT yet landed. No named USG/BARDA contract, no order announcement this week. The catalyst's demand leg remains pending, so thesis stays live. SIGA remains the only pure-play vehicle for TPOXX procurement. The clock is ticking, however — without a concrete order, this degrades into pure declaration-hype, which would not justify a hold.
SIGA.exit_advice: Hold, but watch for a decay-driven exit: if no BARDA/USG named TPOXX order lands within a few weeks, the declaration-only catalyst is spent and the sympathy rally fades.

EBS.thesis: Mpox emergency boosts vaccine/countermeasure maker shares
EBS.exit_case: WHO emergency declared Aug 14-15 already triggered the share spike (EBS/peers jumped); the news event is public and priced.
EBS.assessment: Entry week. WHO declared mpox a public health emergency of international concern (Aug 14-15), sparking a broad rally in countermeasure makers — EBS, Bavarian Nordic, SIGA, GeoVax. The declaration itself is the trigger but is not the resolution: the durable driver is the procurement/order cycle that follows a declared emergency, which is still pending. EBS (ACAM2000, smallpox/mpox countermeasures) is the known vehicle and a reasonable pure-ish play, though Bavarian Nordic (Jynneos) is the cleaner mpox-vaccine name if it enters the known set. Holding on the active, unresolved emergency.
EBS.exit_advice: Hold while the declared emergency is active and government vaccine-procurement orders remain pending; exit if contracts get placed/denied or WHO de-escalates.
